spec_match.rationale: Shows the before / after dialog states the chromestatus summary describes — pre-142 dialog displays only the top frame, 142 dialog adds the iframe origin alongside it.

interactive.rationale: Three text inputs (top site, iframe origin, IDP) drive two live mocked FedCM dialogs side-by-side so users can edit and see the change.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature blurb sells 'edit the top-level site, the embedded iframe, and the identity provider — see the FedCM consent dialog before vs Chrome 142' — page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers the happy A/B compare. Missing: same-origin iframe (where the new behaviour should NOT change the dialog), iframe blocked by Permissions-Policy identity-credentials-get, and IDP-side rejection (configURL fetch fails).

references.rationale: Links chromestatus.com/feature/5176474637959168 in the see-also list.

design_system.rationale: Mocked FedCM dialog uses --bg-paper / --border-black / --accent-blue button; dialog buttons override both background AND color (rule 6); no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Show the same-origin iframe case
openQuestions.detail: When an iframe is same-origin (foo.com embedding foo.com/widget) the spec says the new behaviour should NOT trigger because there's nothing meaningfully third-party. Add a 'same-origin embed' preset that demonstrates the dialog stays unchanged — this is the carve-out the chromestatus summary mentions and authors will want to confirm.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate Permissions-Policy gating
openQuestions.detail: navigator.credentials.get from a 3p iframe is governed by Permissions-Policy: identity-credentials-get. Without an allow=identity-credentials-get on the iframe element, the call rejects. The sibling embed-matrix concept covers this; this page should at least show the rejection path so authors don't paste the code into an iframe that lacks the policy and wonder why nothing happens.

openQuestions.title: Add IDP origin to the dialog mock
openQuestions.detail: The spec PR adds the iframe origin alongside top frame AND the IDP origin. The current mock shows top + iframe; surface the IDP host too (e.g. 'accounts.google.com asks to share account with payments.stripe.com on news.com') so the full three-party disclosure matches the spec text.

summary: Solid before/after dialog comparator with editable inputs. To match the full spec story, add the same-origin carve-out, the Permissions-Policy gate, and the IDP origin in the dialog mock.
